title: The Invisible Boy
author: Trudy Ludwig
shortDescription: A quiet boy learns that small acts of kindness can make someone feel seen.
shellySays: If you ever felt left out, this book shows how small kindness can make a big difference.
whyRecommended: Helps children who feel invisible or left out understand that a single kind gesture can change everything.
fullDescription: Brian is the invisible boy. Nobody ever seems to notice him or think to include him in their group, game, or birthday party — until a new kid arrives and Brian is the first to make him feel welcome.

title: Dragons Love Tacos
author: Adam Rubin
shortDescription: Dragons love tacos — but watch out for the spicy salsa!
shellySays: If you like funny surprises and dragons, this story will make you laugh!
whyRecommended: A laugh-out-loud story that gets reluctant readers excited about books through pure silliness.
fullDescription: Did you know that dragons love tacos? They love chicken tacos, beef tacos, great big tacos, and teeny tiny tacos. But be careful — never feed a dragon a taco with spicy salsa.

title: The Book With No Pictures
author: B.J. Novak
shortDescription: A book that makes the grown-up reading it say the silliest things.
shellySays: This book makes your parents say ridiculous words. It’s very funny!
whyRecommended: Empowers kids by flipping the script — the adult has to read whatever the book says, no matter how silly.
fullDescription: You might think a book with no pictures seems boring and serious. But this book is different — the person reading it has to say every single word, even if those words are ridiculous.

title: Wonder
author: R.J. Palacio
shortDescription: Auggie was born with a facial difference and navigates his first year at school.
shellySays: This book shows how being kind can change someone’s whole world.
whyRecommended: A powerful reminder that choosing kindness — especially when it’s hard — can change someone’s whole world.
fullDescription: August Pullman was born with a facial difference that prevented him from going to mainstream school. Starting 5th grade at Beecher Prep, he wants nothing more than to be treated as an ordinary kid — but his classmates can’t get past his face.

title: The Wild Robot
author: Peter Brown
shortDescription: A robot washes ashore on a wild island and must learn to survive among animals.
shellySays: A robot learns how to survive and make friends in the wild.
whyRecommended: An exciting survival adventure that explores what it means to belong and the courage to adapt to a completely new world.
fullDescription: When robot Roz opens her eyes for the first time, she discovers that she is alone on a remote, wild island. She has no idea how she got there or what her purpose is. As Roz slowly learns to survive, she realizes she must adapt to her harsh surroundings and the animals that are afraid of her.

title: Dog Man
author: Dav Pilkey
shortDescription: Part dog, part policeman — all hero. A hilarious comic-style adventure.
shellySays: If you love silly heroes and comics, Dog Man is awesome.
whyRecommended: A gateway book for reluctant readers — the comic format and non-stop humor make reading irresistible.
fullDescription: When a cop and a police dog are injured in the line of duty, the doctor has a bold idea — he sews the dog’s head onto the cop’s body. Dog Man is born, and he’s the best of both worlds.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
shortDescription: A boy discovers he’s a wizard and enters a magical boarding school.
shellySays: A boy discovers he’s a wizard and enters a magical school.
whyRecommended: The ultimate gateway to reading for adventure-loving kids — a world of friendship, bravery, and magic.
fullDescription: Harry Potter has never even heard of Hogwarts when the letters start dropping on the doormat at number four, Privet Drive. Then, on Harry’s eleventh birthday, a great beetle-eyed giant of a man called Rubeus Hagrid bursts in with some astonishing news: Harry Potter is a wizard.

title: Percy Jackson and the Lightning Thief
author: Rick Riordan
shortDescription: Percy discovers he’s a demigod and goes on a quest to prevent a war among the gods.
shellySays: Percy learns he’s a demigod and goes on a dangerous quest.
whyRecommended: Makes ancient mythology feel alive and funny — perfect for kids who want action, humor, and a hero who doesn’t take himself too seriously.
fullDescription: Percy Jackson is a good kid, but he can’t seem to focus on his schoolwork or control his temper. When his mom tells him the truth about where he came from, he learns he’s a demigod — the son of Poseidon, god of the sea.

title: New Kid
author: Jerry Craft
shortDescription: A graphic novel about navigating a new school where he’s one of the few kids of color.
shellySays: A funny and honest story about fitting in at a new school.
whyRecommended: A relatable, award-winning graphic novel about fitting in, staying true to yourself, and the courage to be different.
fullDescription: Seventh grader Jordan Banks loves nothing more than drawing cartoons about his life. But instead of sending him to the art school of his dreams, his parents enroll him in a prestigious private school where he is one of the few kids of color.
